source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus seeing the multitudes went up into the mountain, and when He was set, His disciples came unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He opened His mouth, and taught them saying, Blessed,” etc.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how unambitious He was, and void of boasting: in that He did not lead people about with Him, but whereas, when healing was required, He had Himself gone about everywhere, visiting both towns and country places; now when the multitude is become very great, He sits in one spot: and that not in the midst of any city or forum, but on a mountain and in a wilderness; instructing us to do nothing for display, and to separate ourselves from the tumults of ordinary life,and this most especially, when we are to study wisdom, and to discourse of things needful to be done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when He had gone up into the mount, and “was set down, His disciples came unto Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou their growth in virtue? and how in a momentthey became better men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since the multitude were but gazers on the miracles, but these from that hour desired also to hear some great and high thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed this it was set Him on His teaching, and made Him begin this discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was not men’s bodies only that He was healing, but He was also amending their souls; and again from the care of these He would pass to attendance on the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He at once varied the succor that He gave, and likewise mingled with the instruction afforded by His words, the manifestation of His glory from His works; and besides, He stopped the shameless mouths of the heretics, signifying by this His care of both parts of our being, that He Himself is the Maker of the whole creation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also on each nature He bestowed abundant providence, now amending the one, now the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in this way He was then employed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is said, that “He opened His mouth, and taught them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore is the clause added, “He opened His mouth”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To inform thee that in His very silence He gave instruction, and not when He spoke only: but at one time by “opening His mouth,” at another uttering His voice by the works which He did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when thou hearest that He taught them, do not think of Him as discoursing with His disciples only, but rather with all through them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since the multitude was such as a multitude ever is,and consisted moreover of such as creep on the ground, He withdraws the choir of His disciples, and makes His discourse unto them: in His conversation with them providing that the rest also, who were yet very far from the level of His sayings, might find His lesson of self-denial no longer grievous unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of which indeed both Luke gave intimation, when he said, that He directed His words unto them:and Matthew too, clearly declaring the same, wrote, “His disciples came unto Him, and He taught them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus the others also were sure to be more eagerly attentive to Him, than they would have been, had He addressed Himself unto all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then doth He begin? and what kind of foundations of His new polity doth He lay for us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us hearken with strict attention unto what is said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though it was spoken unto them, it was written for the sake also of all men afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And accordingly on this account, though He had His disciples in His mind in His public preaching, yet unto them He limits not His sayings, but applies all His words of blessing without restriction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He said not, “Blessed are ye, if ye become poor,” but “Blessed are the poor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I may add that even if He had spoken of them, the advice would still be common to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so, when He saith, “Lo!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I am with you always, even unto the end of the world,” He is discoursing not with them only, but also, through them, with all the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in pronouncing them blessed, who are persecuted, and chased, and suffer all intolerable things; not for them only, but also for all who arrive at the same excellency, He weaves His crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, that this may be yet plainer, and to inform thee that thou hast great interest in His sayings, and so indeed hath all mankind, if any choose to give heed; hear how He begins these wondrous words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are the poor in spirit; for theirs is the kingdom of Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is meant by “the poor in spirit?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The humble and contrite in mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by “spirit” He hath here designated the soul, and the faculty of choice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, since many are humble not willingly, but compelled by stress of circumstances; letting these pass (for this were no matter of praise), He blesses them first, who by choice humble and contract themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why said he not, “the humble,” but rather “the poor?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this is more than that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He means here them who are awestruck, and tremble at the commandments of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whom also by His prophet Isaiah God earnestly accepting said, “To whom will I look, but to him who is meekand quiet, and trembleth at My words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed there are many kinds of humility: one is humble in his own measure, another with all excess of lowliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is this last lowliness of mind which that blessed prophet commends, picturing to us the temper that is not merely subdued, but utterly broken, when he saith, “The sacrifice for God is a contrite spirit, a contrite and an humble heart God will not despise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the Three Children also offer this unto God as a great sacrifice, saying, “Nevertheless, in a contrite soul, and in a spirit of lowliness, may we be accepted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This Christ also now blesses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas the greatest of evils, and those which make havoc of the whole world, had their entering in from pride:—for both the devil, not being such before, did thus become a devil; as indeed Paul plainly declared, saying, “Lest being lifted up with pride, he fall into the condemnation of the devil:”—and the first man, too, puffed up by the devil with these hopes, was made an example of,and became mortal (for expecting to become a god, he lost even what he had; and God also upbraiding him with this, and mocking his folly, said, “Behold, Adam is become as one of us”; and each one of those that came after did hereby wreck himself in impiety, fancying some equality with God:—since, I say, this was the stronghold of our evils, and the root and fountain of all wickedness, He, preparing a remedy suitable to the disease, laid this law first as a strong and safe foundation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this being fixed as a base, the builder in security lays on it all the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if this be taken away, though a man reach to the Heavens in his course of life,it is all easily undermined, and issues in a grievous end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though fasting, prayer, almsgiving, temperance, any other good thing whatever, be gathered together in thee; without humility all fall away and perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was this very thing that took place in the instance of the Pharisee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even after he had arrived at the very summit, he “went down”with the loss of all, because he had not the mother of virtues: for as pride is the fountain of all wickedness, so is humility the principle of all self-command.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He begins with this, pulling up boasting by the very root out of the soul of His hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And what,” one may ask, “is this to His disciples, who were on every account humble?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth they had nothing to be proud of, being fishermen, poor, ignoble, and illiterate.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even though these things concerned not His disciples, yet surely they concerned such as were then present, and such as were hereafter to receive the disciples, lest they should on this account despise them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it were truer to say that they did also concern His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even if not then, yet by and by they were sure to require this help, after their signs and wonders, and their honor from the world, and their confidence towards God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither wealth, nor power, nor royalty itself, had so much power to exalt men, as the things which they possessed in all fullness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, it was natural that even before the signs they might be lifted up, at that very time when they saw the multitude, and all that audience surrounding their Master; they might feel some human weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He at once represses their pride.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He doth not introduce what He saith by way of advice or of commandments, but by way of blessing, so making His word less burthensome, and opening to all the course of His discipline.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “This or that person,” but “they who do so, are all of them blessed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that though thou be a slave, a beggar, in poverty, a stranger, unlearned,there is nothing to hinder thee from being blessed, if thou emulate this virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now having begun, as you see, where most need was, He proceeds to another commandment, one which seems to be opposed to the judgment of the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas all think that they who rejoice are enviable, those in dejection, poverty, and mourning, wretched, He calls these blessed rather than those; saying thus,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that mourn.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely all men call them miserable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For therefore He wrought the miracles beforehand, that in such enactments as these He might be entitled to credit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here too again he designated not simply all that mourn, but all that do so for sins: since surely that other kind of mourning is forbidden, and that earnestly, which relates to anything of this life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This Paul also clearly declared, when he said, “The sorrow of the world worketh death, but godly sorrow worketh repentance unto salvation, not to be repented of.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These then He too Himself calls blessed, whose sorrow is of that kind; yet not simply them that sorrow did He designate, but them that sorrow intensely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He did not say, “they that sorrow,” but “they that mourn.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this commandment again is fitted to teach us entire self-control.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if those who grieve for children, or wife, or any other relation gone from them, have no fondness for gain or pleasure during that period of their sorrow; if they aim not at glory, are not provoked by insults, nor led captive by envy, nor beset by any other passion, their grief alone wholly possessing them; much more will they who mourn for their own sins, as they ought to mourn, show forth a self-denial greater than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Next, what is the reward for these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they shall be comforted,” saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where shall they be comforted! tell me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Both here and there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since the thing enjoined was exceeding burthensome and galling, He promised to give that, which most of all made it light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, if thou wilt be comforted, mourn: and think not this a dark saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when God doth comfort, though sorrows come upon thee by thousands like snow-flakes, thou wilt be above them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since in truth, as the returns which God gives are always far greater than our labors; so He hath wrought in this case, declaring them that mourn to be blessed, not after the value of what they do, but after His own love towards man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they that mourn, mourn for misdoings, and to such it is enough to enjoy forgiveness, and obtain wherewith to answer for themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But forasmuch as He is full of love towards man, He doth not limit His recompense either to the removal of our punishments, or to the deliverance from our sins, but He makes them even blessed, and imparts to them abundant consolation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He bids us mourn, not only for our own, but also for other men’s misdoings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of this temper were the souls of the saints: such was that of Moses, of Paul, of David; yea, all these many times mourned for evils not their own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Tell me, what kind of earth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Somesay a figurative earth, but it is not this, for nowhere in Scripture do we find any mention of an earth that is merely figurative.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what can the saying mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He holds out a sensible prize; even as Paul also doth, in that when he had said, “Honor thy father and thy mother,”he added, “For so shalt thou live long upon the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He Himself unto the thief again, “Today shalt thou be with me in Paradise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He doth not incite us by means of the future blessings only, but of the present also, for the sake of the grosser sort of His hearers, and such as before the future seek those others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, for example, further on also He said, “Agree with thine adversary.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He appoints the reward of such self-command, and saith, “Lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge to the officer.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou whereby He alarmed us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By the things of sense, by what happens before our eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “Whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul too sets forth sensible rewards at great length, and uses things present in his exhortations; as when he is discoursing about virginity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having said nothing about the heavens there, for the time he urges it by things present, saying, “Because of the present distress,” and, “But I spare you,” and, “I would have you without carefulness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus accordingly Christ also with the things spiritual hath mingled the sensible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas the meek man is thought to lose all his own, He promises the contrary, saying, “Nay, but this is he who possesses his goods in safety, namely, he who is not rash, nor boastful: while that sort of man shall often lose his patrimony, and his very life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, since in the Old Testament the prophet used to say continually, “The meek shall inherit the earth;” He thus weaves into His discourse the words to which they were accustomed, so as not everywhere to speak a strange language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He saith, not as limiting the rewards to things present, but as joining with these the other sort of gifts also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither in speaking of any spiritual thing doth He exclude such as are in the present life; nor again in promising such as are in our life, doth He limit his promise to that kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He saith, “Seek ye the kingdom of God, and all these things shall be added unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again: “Whosoever hath left houses or brethren, shall receive an hundred fold in this world, and in the future shall inherit everlasting life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sort of righteousness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He means either the whole of virtue, or that particular virtue which is opposed to covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He is about to give commandment concerning mercy, to show how we must show mercy, as, for instance, not of rapine or covetousness, He blesses them that lay hold of righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see with what exceeding force He puts it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “Blessed are they which keep fast by righteousness,” but, “Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness:” that not merely anyhow, but with all desire we may pursue it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since this is the most peculiar property of covetousness, and we are not so enamored of meat and drink, as of gaining, and compassing ourselves with more and more, He bade us to transfer this desire to a new object, freedom from covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He appoints the prize, again from things sensible; saying, “for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, because it is thought that the rich are commonly made such by covetousness, “Nay,” saith He, “it is just contrary: for it is righteousness that doeth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, so long as thou doest righteously, fear not poverty, nor tremble at hunger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the extortioners, they are the very persons who lose all, even as he certainly who is in love with righteousness, possesses himself the goods of all men in safety.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if they who covet not other men’s goods enjoy so great abundance,much more they who give up their own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are the merciful.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here He seems to me to speak not of those only who show mercy in giving of money, but those likewise who are merciful in their actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the way of showing mercy is manifold, and this commandment is broad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the reward thereof?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they shall obtain mercy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it seems indeed to be a sort of equal recompence, but it is a far greater thing than the act of goodness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas they themselves show mercy as men, they obtain mercy from the God of all; and it is not the same thing, man’s mercy, and God’s; but as wide as is the interval between wickedness and goodness, so far is the one of these removed from the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold again the reward is spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now He here calls “pure,” either those who have attained unto all virtue, and are not conscious to themselves of any evil; or those who live in temperance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is nothing which we need so much in order to see God, as this last virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also said, “Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He is here speaking of such sight as it is possible for man to have.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because there are many who show mercy, and who commit no rapine, nor are covetous, who yet are guilty of fornication and uncleanness; to signify that the former alone suffices not, He hath added this, much in the same sense as Paul, writing to the Corinthians, bore witness of the Macedonians, that they were rich not only in almsgiving, but also in all other virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having spoken of the noble spiritthey had shown in regard of their goods, he saith, “They gave also their own selves to the Lord, and to us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are the peace-makers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here He not only takes away altogether our own strife and hatred amongst ourselves, but He requires besides this something more, namely, that we should set at one again others, who are at strife.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, the reward which He annexes is spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of what kind then is it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they shall be called the children of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for this became the work of the Only Begotten, to unite the divided, and to reconcile the alienated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, lest thou shouldest imagine peace in all cases a blessing, He hath added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, for virtue’s sake, for succorgiven to others, and for godliness: it being ever His wont to call by the name of “righteousness” the whole practical wisdom of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you and persecute you, and say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Rejoice, and be exceeding glad.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As if He said, “Though they should call you sorcerers, deceivers, pestilent persons, or whatever else, blessed are ye:” so He speaks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What could be newer than these injunctions? wherein the very things which all others avoid, these He declares to be desirable; I mean, being poor, mourning, persecution, evil report.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But yet He both affirmed this, and convinced not two, nor ten, nor twenty, nor an hundred, nor a thousand men, but the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And hearing things so grievous and galling, so contrary to the accustomed ways of men, the multitudes “were astonished.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great was the power of Him who spake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, lest thou shouldest think that the mere fact of being evil spoken of makes men blessed, He hath set two limitations; when it is for His sake, and when the things that are said are false: for without these, he who is evil spoken of, so far from being blessed, is miserable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then see the prize again: “Because your reward is great in heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, though thou hear not of a kingdom given in each one of the blessings, be not discouraged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although He give different names to the rewards, yet He brings all into His kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, both when He saith, “they that mourn shall be comforted;” and, “they that show mercy shall obtain mercy;” and, “the pure in heart shall see God;” and, the peacemakers “shall be called the children of God;” nothing else but the Kingdom doth He shadow out by all these sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such as enjoy these, shall surely attain unto that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Think not therefore that this reward is for the poor in spirit only, but for those also who hunger after righteousness, for the meek, and for all the rest without exception.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since on this account He hath set His blessing on them all, that thou mightest not look for anything sensible: for that man cannot be blessed, who is crowned with such things as come to an end with this present life, and hurry by quicker than a shadow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when He had said, “your reward is great,” he added also another consolation, saying, “For so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, since that first, the promise of the Kingdom, was yet to come, and all in expectation, He affords them comfort from this world; from their fellowship with those who before them had been ill-treated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “think not,” saith He, “that for something inconsistent in your sayings and enactments ye suffer these things: or, as being teachers of evil doctrines, ye are to be persecuted by them; the plots and dangers proceed not of any wickedness in your sayings, but of the malice of those who hear you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither are they any blame to you who suffer wrong, but to them who do the wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to the truth of these things all preceding time bears witness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For against the prophets they did not even bring any charge of transgressing the law, and of sentiments of impiety, that they stoned some, chased away others, encompassed others with innumerable afflictions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore let not this trouble you, for of the very same mind they do all that is done now.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He raised up their spirits, by placing them near to the company of Moses and Elias?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also Paul writing to the Thessalonians, saith, “For ye became followers of the Churches of God, which are in Judea; for ye also have suffered the same things of your own fellow-countrymen, even as they have of the Jews: who both killed the Lord Jesus, and their own prophets, and have driven us out; and they please not God, and are contrary to all men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which same point here also Christ hath established.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas in the other beatitudes, He said, “Blessed are the poor,” and “the merciful;” here He hath not put it generally, but addresses His speech unto themselves, saying, “Blessed are ye, when they shall revile you, and persecute you, and say every evil word:” signifying that this is an especial privilege of theirs; and that beyond all others, teachers have this for their own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At the same time He here also covertly signifies His own dignity, and His equality in honor with Him who begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “as they on the Father’s account,” saith He, “so shall ye also for me suffer these things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when He saith, “the prophets which were before you,” He implies that they were also by this time become prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Next, declaring that this above all profits them, and makes them glorious, He did not say, “they will calumniate and persecute you, but I will prevent it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not in their escaping evil report, but in their noble endurance thereof, and in refuting them by their actions, He will have their safety stand: this being a much greater thing than the other; even as to be struck and not hurt, is much greater than escaping the blow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now in this place He saith, “Your reward is great in heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Lukereports Him to have spoken this, both earnestly, and with more entire consolation; for He not only, as you know, pronounces them blessed, who are evil spoken of for God’s sake, but declares them likewise wretched, who are well spoken of by all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Woe unto you,” saith He, “when all men shall speak well of you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet the apostles were well spoken of, but not by all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He said not, “Woe unto you, when men shall speak well of you,” but, “when all men” shall do so: for it is not even possible that those who live in the practice of virtue should be well spoken of by all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again He saith, “When they shall cast out your name as evil, rejoice ye, and leap for joy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only of the dangers they underwent, but of the calumny also, He appoints the recompence to be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He said not, “When they shall persecute, and kill you,” but, “When they shall revile you, and say all manner of evil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For most assuredly, men’s evil reports have a sharper bite than their very deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas, in our dangers, there are many things that lighten the toil, as to be cheeredby all, to have many to applaud, to crown, to proclaim our praise; here in our reproach even this consolation is destroyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because we seem not to have achieved anything great; and this galls the combatant more than all his dangers: at least many have gone on even to hang themselves, not bearing evil report.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why marvellest thou at the others? since that traitor, that shameless and accursed one, he who had ceased to blush for anything whatever, was wrought upon by this chiefly to hurry to the halter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Job again, all adamant as he was, and firmer than a rock; when he had been robbed of all his possessions, and was suffering those incurable ills, and had become on a sudden childless, and when he saw his body pouring out worms like a fountain, and his wife attacking him, he repelled it all with ease; but when he saw his friends reproaching and trampling upon him, and entertaining an evil opinion of him, and saying that he suffered those things for some sins, and was paying the penalty of wickedness: then was there trouble, then commotion, even in that great and noble-hearted man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And David also, letting pass all that he had suffered, sought of God a retribution for the calumny alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Let him curse,” saith he, “for the Lord hath bidden him: that the Lord may see my humiliation, and requite me for this cursing of his on this day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul too proclaims the triumph not of those only who incur danger, or are deprived of their goods, but of these also, thus saying, “Call to remembrance the former days, in which after ye were illuminated ye endured a great fight of afflictions; partly whilst ye were made a gazing stock by reproaches, and afflictions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On this account then Christ hath appointed the reward also to be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, lest any one should say, “Here thou givest no redress, nor stoppest men’s mouths; and dost thou assign a reward there?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He hath put before us the prophets, to show that neither in their case did God give redress.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if, where the rewards were at hand, He cheered them with things to come; much more now, when this hope is become clearer, and self-denial is increased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And observe too, after how many commandments He hath put this, for surely He did it not without reason, but to show that it is not possible for one unprovided, and unarmed with all those other virtues, to go forth unto these conflicts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, you see, in each instance, by the former precept making way for the following one, He hath woven a sort of golden chain for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, first, he that is “humble,” will surely also “mourn” for his own sins: he that so “mourns,” will be both “meek,” and “righteous,” and “merciful;” he that is “merciful,” and “righteous,” and “contrite” will of course be also “pure in heart:” and such a one will be “a peacemaker” too: and he that hath attained unto all these, will be moreover arrayed against dangers, and will not be troubled when evil is spoken of him, and he is enduring grievous trials innumerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now then, after giving them due exhortation, He refreshes them again with praises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As thus: the injunctions being high, and far surpassing those in the Old Testament; lest they should be disturbed and confounded, and say, “How shall we be able to achieve these things?” hear what He saith: “Ye are the salt of the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Implying, that of absolute necessity He enjoins all this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “not for your own life apart,” saith He, “but for the whole world, shall your account be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not to two cities, nor to ten or twenty, nor to a single nation am I sending you, as I sent the prophets; but to earth, and sea, and the whole world; and that in evil case.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by saying, “Ye are the salt of the earth,” He signified all human nature to have “lost its savor,”and to be decayed by our sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which cause, you see, He requires of them such virtues, as are most necessary and useful for the superintendence of the common sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For first, the meek, and yielding, and merciful, and righteous, shuts not up his good deeds unto himself only, but also provides that these good fountains should run over for the benefit of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he again who is pure in heart, and a peacemaker, and is persecuted for the truth’s sake; he again orders his way of life for the common good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Think not then,” He saith, “that ye are drawn on to ordinary conflicts, or that for some small matters you are to give account.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye are the salt of the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then? did they restore the decayed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means; for neither is it possible to do any good to that which is already spoilt, by sprinkling it with salt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This therefore they did not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But rather, what things had been before restored, and committed to their charge, and freed from that ill savor, these they then salted, maintaining and preserving them in that freshness,which they had received of the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that men should be set free from the rottenness of their sins was the good work of Christ; but their not returning to it again any more was the object of these men’s diligence and travail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how by degrees He indicates their superiority to the very prophets? in that He saith they are teachers, not of Palestine, but of the whole world; and not simply teachers, but awful ones too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is the marvellous thing, that not by flattering, nor soothing, but by sharply bracingthem, as salt, even so they became dear to all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now marvel not,” saith He, “if leaving all others, I discourse to you, and draw you on to so great dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For consider over how many cities, tribes, and nations, I am to send you to preside.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I would have you not only be prudent yourselves, but that you should also make others the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And such persons have great need to be intelligent, in whom the salvation of the rest is at stake: they ought so much to abound in virtue, as to impart of the profit to others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if ye do not become such as this, ye will not suffice even for your own selves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Be not then impatient, as though my sayings were too burdensome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while it is possible for others who have lost their savor to return by your means, you, if you should come to this, will with yourselves destroy others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that in proportion as the matters are great, which ye have put into your hands, you need so much the greater diligence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But if the salt have lost its savor, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For other men, though they fall never so often, may possibly obtain indulgence: but the teacher, should this happen to him, is deprived of all excuse, and will suffer the most extreme vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, lest at the words, “When they shall revile you, and persecute you, and say all manner of evil against you,” they should be too timid to go forth: He tells them, “unless ye are prepared to combat with all this, ye have been chosen in vain.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not evil report that ye should fear, but lest ye should prove partners in dissimulation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then, “Ye will lose your savor, and be trodden under foot:” but if ye continue sharply to brace them up, and then are evil spoken of, rejoice; for this is the very use of salt, to sting the corrupt,and make them smart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And so their censure follows of course, in no way harming you, but rather testifying your firmness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if through fear of it you give up the earnestness that becomes you, ye will have to suffer much more grievously, being both evil spoken of, and despised by all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is the meaning of “trodden under foot.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this He leads on to another, a higher image.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye are the light of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Of the world” again; not of one nation, nor of twenty states,but of the whole inhabited earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And “a light” to the mind, far better than this sunbeam: like as they were also a spiritual salt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And before they are salt, and now light; to teach thee how great is the gain of these strictprecepts, and the profit of that grave discipline: how it binds, and permits not to become dissolute; and causes clear sight, leading men on to virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “A city that is set on a hill cannot be hid, neither do men light a candle, and put it under the bushel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, by these words He trains them to strictness of life, teaching them to be earnest in their endeavors, as set before the eyes of all men, and contending in the midst of the amphitheatre of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “look not to this,” He saith, “that we are now sitting here, that we are in a small portion of one corner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For ye shall be as conspicuous to all as a city set on the ridge of a hill, as a candle in a house on the candlestick, giving light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where now are they who persevere in disbelieving the power of Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let them hear these things, and let them adore His might, amazed at the power of the prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For consider how great things he promised to them, who were not known even in their own country: that earth and sea should know them, and that they should by their fame reach to the limits of the inhabited world; or rather, not by their fame, but by the working of the good they wrought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was not fame that bearing them everywhere made them conspicuous, but also the actual demonstration by their works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since, as though they had wings, more vehemently than the sunbeam did they overrun the whole earth, sowing the light of godliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But here He seems to me to be also training them to boldness of speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to say, “A city set on a hill cannot be hid,” is to speak as declaring His own powers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as that city can by no means be hidden, so it was impossible that what they preached should sink into silence and obscurity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, since He had spoken of persecutions and calumnies, of plots and wars, for fear they might think that these would have power to stop their mouths; to encourage them, He saith, that so far from being hid, it should over-shine the whole world; and that on this very account they should be illustrious and renowned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By this then He declares His own power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In what follows, He requires that boldness of speech which was due on their part; thus saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Neither do men light a candle and put it under the bushel, but on the candlestick, and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I,” saith He, “it is true, have kindled the light, but its continuing to burn, let that come of your diligence: not for your own sakes alone, but also for their sake, who are to profit by these rays, and to be guided unto the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since the calumnies surely shall not be able to obscure your brightness, if you be still living a strict life, and as becomes those who are to convert the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Show forth therefore a life worthy of His grace; that even as it is everywhere preached, so this light may everywhere accompany the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Next He sets before them another sort of gain, besides the salvation of mankind, enough to make them strive earnestly, and to lead them unto all diligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As thus, “Ye shall not only,” saith He, “amend the world, if ye live aright, but ye will also give occasion that God shall be glorified; even as if ye do the contrary, ye will both destroy men, and make God’s name to be blasphemed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how, it may be asked, shall God be glorified through us, if at least men are to speak evil of us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, not all men, and even they themselves who in envy do this, will in their conscience admire and approve you; even as the outward flatterers of such as live in wickedness do in mind accuse them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou command us to live for display and vain glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Far from it; I say not this; for I did not say, “Give ye diligence to bring forward your own good deeds,” neither did I say, “Show them;” but “Let your light shine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, “Let your virtue be great, and the fire abundant, and the light unspeakable.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when virtue is so great, it cannot lie hid, though its pursuer shade it over ten thousand fold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Present unto them an irreprehensible life, and let them have no true occasion of evil speaking; and then, though there be thousands of evil-speakers, no man shall be able to cast any shade upon you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And well did He say, “your light,” for nothing makes a man so illustrious, how manifold soever his will to be concealed, as the manifestation of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as if he were clad with the very sunbeam, so he shines, yet brighter than it; not spending his rays on earth, but surmounting also Heaven itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence also He comforts them more abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “What though the slander pain you,” saith He; “yet shall ye have many to honor God on your account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in both ways your recompence is gathering, as well because God is glorified through you, as because ye are defamed for God’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, lest we should on purpose seek to be reproached, on hearing that there is a reward for it: first, He hath not expressed that sentiment simply, but with two limitations, namely, when what is said is false, and when it is for God’s sake:—and next He signifies how not that only, but also good report, hath its great profit, the glory of it passing on to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He holds out to them those gracious hopes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For,” saith He, “the calumny of the wicked avails not so much as to put all others in the dark, in respect of seeing your light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then only when you have “lost your savor” shall they tread you under foot; but not when you are falsely accused, doing right.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, rather then shall there be many admiring, not you only, but for your sake your Father also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not “God,” but “your Father;” already sowing beforehand the seeds of that noble birth, which was about to be bestowed upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Moreover, indicating His parity in honor, as He said above, “Grieve not when ye are evil spoken of, for it is enough for you that for my sake you are thus spoken of;” so here He mentions the Father: every where manifesting His equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then we know the gain that arises from this earnestness, and the danger of indolence (for if our Lord be blasphemed because of us, that were far worse than our perdition), let us “give none offense, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the Church of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And while the life which we present before them is brighter than the sun, yet if any one will speak evil of us, let us not grieve at being defamed, but only if we be defamed with justice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, on the one hand, if we live in wickedness, though there be none to speak ill of us, we shall be the most wretched of all men: on the other hand, if we apply ourselves to virtue, though the whole world speak evil of us, at that very time we shall be more enviable than any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And we shall draw on to follow us all who choose to be saved, for not the calumny of the wicked, but our good life, will draw their attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed no trumpet is so clear as the proof that is given by our actions: neither is the light itself so transparent as a pure life, though our calumniators be beyond number.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say, if all the above-mentioned qualities be ours; if we be meek and lowly and merciful; if we be pure, and peacemakers; if hearing reproach, we revile not again, but rather rejoice; then shall we attract all that observe us no less than the miracles do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all will be kindly disposed towards us, though one be a wild beast, a demon, or what you will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or if there should even be some who speak evil of thee, be not thou at all troubled thereat, nor because they revile thee in public, regard it; but search into their conscience, and thou shalt see them applauding and admiring thee, and numbering up ten thousand praises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See, for instance, how Nebuchadnezzar praises the children in the furnace; yet surely he was an adversary and an enemy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But upon seeing them stand nobly, he proclaims their triumph, and crowns them: and that for nought else, but because they disobeyed him, and hearkened unto the law of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the devil, when he sees himself effecting nothing, from that time departs, fearing lest he should be the cause of our winning more crowns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when he is gone, even one who is abominable and depraved will recognize virtue, that mist being withdrawn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or if men still argue perversely, thou shalt have from God the greater praise and admiration.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Grieve not now, I pray thee, neither despond; since the very apostles were to some a “savor of death;”to others, a “savor of life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if there be nothing to lay hold of in thyself, thou art rid of all their charges; or rather, thou art become the more blessed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shine out therefore in thy life, and take no account of them who speak evil of thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it cannot, it cannot be, that one careful of virtue, should not have many enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, this is nothing to the virtuous man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by such means his brightness will increase the more abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then, bearing these things in mind, look to one object only; how to order our own life with strictness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus we shall also guide to the life that is there, such as are now sitting in darkness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the virtue of that light, as not only to shine here, but also to conduct its followers thither.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when men see us despising all things present, and preparing ourselves for that which is to come, our actions will persuade them sooner than any discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who is there so senseless, that at sight of one, who within a day or two was living in luxury and wealth, now stripping himself of all, and putting on wings, and arrayed to meet both hunger and poverty, and all hardship, and dangers, and blood, and slaughter, and everything that is counted dreadful; will not from this sight derive a clear demonstration of the things which are to come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if we entangle ourselves in things present, and plunge ourselves in them more and more, how will it be possible for them to be persuaded that we are hastening to another sojourn?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what excuse after this shall we have, if the fear of God avail not so much with us, as human glory availed with the Greek philosophers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For some of them did really both lay aside wealth, and despised death, that they might make a show before men; wherefore also their hopes became vain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What plea then shall deliver us, when with so great things set before us, and with so high a rule of self-denial laid open to us, we are not able even to do as they did, but ruin both ourselves and others besides?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is the harm so great when a heathen commits transgression, as when a Christian doeth the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of course not; for their character is already lost, but ours, by reason of the grace of God, is even among the ungodly venerable and glorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore when they would most revile us, and aggravate their evil speech, they add some such taunt as, “Thou Christian:” a taunt which they would not utter, did they not secretly entertain a great opinion of our doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard how many, and how great precepts Christ enjoined?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now when wilt thou be able to fulfill one of those commandments, while thou leavest all, and goest about gathering interest, tacking together usuries, setting on foot transactions of business, buying herds of slaves, procuring silver vessels, purchasing houses, fields, goods without end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I would this were all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when to these unseasonable pursuits, thou addest even injustice, removing landmarks,taking away houses by violence, aggravating poverty, increasing hunger, when wilt thou be able to set thy foot on these thresholds?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But sometimes thou showest mercy to the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I know it as well as thou.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But even in this again great is the mischief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou doest this either in pride or in vainglory, so as not to profit even by thy good deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What can be more wretched than this, to be making thy shipwreck in the very harbor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To prevent this, when thou hast done any good action, seek not thanks from me, that thou mayest have God thy debtor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Lend,” saith He, “unto them from whom ye do not expect to receive.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou hast thy Debtor; why leave Him, and require it of me, a poor and wretched mortal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? is that Debtor displeased, when the debt is required of Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? is He poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is He unwilling to pay?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not His unspeakable treasures?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not His indescribable munificence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lay hold then on Him, and make thy demand; for He is pleased when one thus demands the debt of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, if He see another required to pay for what He Himself owes, He will feel as though He were insulted, and repay thee no more; nay, He justly finds fault, saying, “Why, of what ingratitude hast thou convicted me? what poverty dost thou know to be in me, that thou hastenest by me, and resortest unto others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou lent to One, and dost thou demand the debt of another?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although man received it, it was God that commanded thee to bestow; and His will is to be Himself, and in the original sense,debtor, and surety, affording thee ten thousand occasion to demand the debt of Him from every quarter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not thou then let go so great facility and abundance, and seek to receive of me who have nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, to what end dost thou display to me thy mercy shown to the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What! was it I that said to thee, Give? was it from me that thou didst hear this; that thou shouldest demand it back of me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He Himself hath said, “He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou hast lent to God:put it to His account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But He doth not repay the whole now.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well, this too He doth for thy good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such a debtor is He: not as many, who are anxious simply to repay that which is lent; whereas He manages and doeth all things, with a view of investing likewise in security that which hath been given unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore some, you see, He repays here: some He assignsin the other place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing therefore as we do these things, let us make our mercifulness abundant, let us give proof of much love to man, both by the use of our money, and by our actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if we see any one ill-treated and beaten in the market-place, whether we can pay down money, let us do it: or whether by words we may separate them, let us not be backward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even a word has its re ward, and still more have sighs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this the blessed Job said; “But I wept for every helpless one, and I sighed when I saw a man in distress.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if there be a reward for tears and sighs; when words also, and an anxious endeavor, and many things besides are added, consider how great the recompence becomes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for we too were enemies to God, and the Only-begotten reconciled us, casting himself between, and for us receiving stripes, and for us enduring death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then likewise do our diligence to deliver from countless evils such as are incurring them; and not as we now do, when we see any beating and tearing one another: we are apt to stand by, finding pleasure in the disgrace of others, and forming a devilish amphitheatre around: than which what can be more cruel?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou seest men reviled, tearing each other to pieces, rending their clothes, smiting each other’s faces, and dost thou endure to stand by quietly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What! is it a bear that is fighting? a wild beast? a serpent?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is a man, one who hath in every respect fellowship with thee: a brother, a member.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Look not on, but separate them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Take no pleasure, but amend the evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Stir not up others to the shameful sight, but rather drive off and separate those who are assembled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is for shameless persons, and born slaves,to take pleasure in such calamities; for those that are mere refuse, for asses without reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou seest a man behaving himself unseemly, and dost thou not account the unseemliness thine own?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou not interpose, and scatter the devil’s troop, and put an end to men’s miseries?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “That I may receive blows myself,” saith one; “is this also thy bidding?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou wilt not have to suffer even this; but if thou shouldest, the thing would be to thee a sort of martyrdom; for thou didst suffer on God’s behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou art slow to receive blows, consider that thy Lord was not slow to endure the cross for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since they for their part are drunken in darkness; wrath being their tyrant and commander; and they need some one who is sound to help them, both the wrong-doer, and he who is injured; the one that he may be delivered from suffering evil, the other that he may cease to do it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Draw nigh, therefore, and stretch forth the hand, thou that art sober to him that is drunken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is a drunkenness of wrath too, and that more grievous than the drunkenness of wine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not the seamen, how, when they see any meeting with shipwreck, they spread their sails, and set out with all haste, to rescue those of the same craft out of the waves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now, if partakers in an art show so much care one for another, how much more ought they who are partakers of the same nature to do all these things!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because in truth here too is a shipwreck, a more grievous one than that; for either a man under provocation blasphemes, and so throws all away: or he forswears himself under the sway of his wrath, and that way falls into hell: or he strikes a blow and commits murder, and thus again suffers the very same shipwreck.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Go thou then, and put a stop to the evil; pull out them that are drowning, though thou descend into the very depth of the surge; and having broken up the theatre of the devil, take each one of them apart, and admonish him to quell the flame, and to lull the waves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if the burning pile wax greater, and the furnace more grievous, be not thou terrified; for thou hast many to help thee, and stretch forth the hand, if thou furnish but a beginning; and above all thou surely hast with thee the God of peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou wilt first turn aside the flames, many others also will follow, and of what they do well, thou wilt thyself receive the reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear what precept Christ gave to the Jews, creeping as they did upon the earth: “If thou see,” saith He, “thine enemy’s beast of burden falling down, do not hasten by, but raise it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou must see that to separate and reconcile men that are fighting is a much lighter thing than to lift up the fallen beast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if we ought to help in raising our enemies’ ass, much more our friends’ souls: and most when the fall is more grievous; for not into mire do these fall, but into the fire of hell, not bearing the burden of their wrath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou, when thou seest thy brother lying under the load, and the devil standing by, and kindling the pile, thou runnest by, cruelly and unmercifully; a kind of thing not safe to do, even where brutes are concerned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas the Samaritan, seeing a wounded man, unknown, and not at all appertaining to him, both staid, and set him on a beast, and brought him home to the inn, and hired a physician, and gave some money, and promised more: thou, seeing one fallen not among thieves, but amongst a band of demons, and beset by anger; and this not in a wilderness, but in the midst of the forum; not having to lay out money, nor to hire a beast, nor to bring him on a long way, but only to say some words:—art thou slow to do it? and holdest back, and hurriest by cruelly and unmercifully?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how thinkest thou, calling upon God, ever to find Him propitious?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let me speak also to you, who publicly disgrace yourselves: to him who is acting despitefully, and doing wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Art thou inflicting blows? tell me; and kicking, and biting? art thou become a wild boar, and a wild ass? and art thou not ashamed? dost thou not blush at thus being changed into a wild beast, and betraying thine own nobleness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though thou be poor, thou art free; though thou be a working man, thou art a Christian.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, for this very reason, that thou art poor, thou shouldest be quiet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For fightings belong to the rich, not to the poor; to the rich, who have many causes to force them to war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, not having the pleasure of wealth, goest about gathering to thyself the evils of wealth, enmities, and strifes, and fightings; and takest thy brother by the throat, and goest about to strangle him, and throwest him down publicly in the sight of all men: and dost thou not think that thou art thyself rather disgraced, imitating the violent passions of the brutes; nay rather, becoming even worse than they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they have all things in common; they herd one with another, and go about together: but we have nothing in common, but all in confusion: fightings, strifes, revilings, and enmities, and insults.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And we neither reverence the heaven, unto which we are called all of us in common; nor the earth, which He hath left free to us all in common; nor our very nature; but wrath and the love of money sweeps all away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou not seen him who owed the ten thousand talents, and then, after he was forgiven that debt, took his fellow-servant by the throat for an hundred pence, what great evils he underwent, and how he was delivered over to an endless punishment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou not trembled at the example?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou no fear, lest thou too incur the same?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we likewise owe to our Lord many and great debts: nevertheless, He forbears, and suffers long, and neither urges us, as we do our fellow-servants, nor chokes and takes us by the throat; yet surely had he been minded to exact of us but the least part thereof, we had long ago perished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then, beloved, bearing these things in mind, be humbled, and feel thankful to those who are in debt to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they become to us, if we command ourselves, an occasion of obtaining most abundant pardon; and giving a little, we shall receive much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then exact with violence, it being meet, though the other were minded to pay, for thee of thine accord to excuse him, that thou mayest receive the whole of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now thou doest all things, and art violent, and contentious,to have none of thy debts forgiven thee; and whilst thou art thinking to do despite unto thy neighbor, thou art thrusting the sword into thyself, so increasing thy punishment in hell: whereas if thou wilt show a little self-command here, thou makest thine own accounts easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed God therefore wills us to take the lead in that kind of bounty, that He may take occasion to repay us with increase.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As many therefore as stand indebted to thee, either for money, or for trespasses, let them all go free, and require of God the recompense of such thy magnanimity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so long as they continue indebted to thee, thou canst not have God thy debtor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou let them go free, thou wilt be able to detain thy God, and to require of Him the recompense of so great self-restraint in bountiful measure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For suppose a man had come up and seeing thee arresting thy debtor, had called upon thee to let him go free, and transfer to himself thy account with the other: he would not choose to be unfairafter such remission, seeing he had passed the whole demand to himself: how then shall God fail to repay us manifold, yea, ten thousand fold, when for His commandment’s sake, if any be indebted to us, we urge no complaint against them, great or small, but let them go exempt from all liability?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then think of the temporary pleasure that springs up in us by exacting of our debtors, but of the loss, rather, how great! which we shall thereby sustain hereafter, grievously injuring ourselves in the things which are eternal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Rising accordingly above all, let us forgive those who must give account to us, both their debts and their offenses; that we may make our own accounts prove indulgent, and that what we could not reach by all virtue besides, this we may obtain by not bearing malice against our neighbors; and thus enjoy the eternal blessings, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might now and always, even forever and ever.
